attributes.description: Alerts when containers are using more than 90% of their CPU limit. Throttled containers experience increased latency without triggering crashes or OOMKills, making them hard to detect without explicit monitoring.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] Container CPU throttling

investigation_guide.blob:
## Container CPU Throttling

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected container(s) from the alert results.
2. Open the Pod & Container Detail dashboard to see CPU usage vs limits over time.
3. Determine if CPU usage is sustained (undersized limit) or spiking (bursty workload).
4. Check current CPU usage: `kubectl top pods -n <namespace> --containers`.
5. Review the container's CPU limit — is it set appropriately for the workload?

### Common Causes
- CPU limit too low for actual workload requirements
- Bursty workloads that periodically exceed their limit
- Increased traffic or processing load
- Inefficient code or unoptimised queries

### Tuning
- The default threshold is 90% of the CPU limit.
- Lower to 80% for early warning; raise to 95% to reduce noise.
- Exclude known CPU-intensive containers that run close to their limits by design.

### Escalation
Throttled containers cause latency degradation without visible errors. Increase CPU limits or optimise the workload.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-kubeletstatsreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.container.cpu_limit_utilization IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_util = AVG(k8s.container.cpu_limit_utilization)
    BY k8s.pod.name, k8s.container.name, k8s.namespace.name, k8s.node.name
// Alert when average CPU usage exceeds 90% of the container's limit.
// Adjust the 0.9 threshold to tune sensitivity:
//   0.80 = early warning (more alerts)
//   0.95 = heavy throttling (fewer alerts)
| WHERE avg_util > 0.9
| EVAL util_pct = ROUND(avg_util * 100, 1)
| SORT util_pct DESC
| KEEP k8s.namespace.name, k8s.pod.name, k8s.container.name, k8s.node.name, util_pct
| LIMIT 50
